He, the Almighty Allah, said,

which means, “Then what is the matter with you that you are divided into two parties about the hypocrites? Allah has cast

them back (to disbelief} because of what they have earned.”

which means, “And they say, "Our hearts are wrapped (i.e. do not hear or understand Allah's Word)." Nay, Allah has cursed them for their disbelief, so little is that which they believe.” (AlBaqarah, 2:88)

He, the Exalted said,

218 * AL-FAWA'ID: A Collection of Wise Sayings

a7 . > eo ous Po bore te ea 4B). 8 Ol ay La BI US pa pels apes Llaly >

which means, “And We shall turn their hearts and their eyes away (from guidance), as they refused to believe therein for the first time.” (Al-An’am, 6:110)

He, the Exalted, announced that He punished them due to their disbelief when the Prophets came to them with the message. They knew it well, but they just turned away from it, so Allah, the Almighty turned their hearts and their eyes away from guidance, and came in between them and faith. Just as the Almighty said,

< fe ston ws rant 4¢@ oes eek de gk Lash igtlety pea es SES IY Lily ob tat Ite Gall GE > 4@) alls ST ou Ue al

which means, “O you who believe! Answer Allah (by obeying Him) and (His) Messenger when he calls you to that which will give you life, and know that Allah comes in between a person and his heart (ie. He prevents an evil person to decide anything).” (Al-Anafal, 8:24)

He, the Exalted, ordered them to obey Him and His Messenger (peace be upon him) when He called them to that which will give them life. Then He warned them not to fail to obey Him, for this would be the reason for Him to come in between them and their hearts. Allah, the Exalted said,

which means, “So when they turned away (from the Path of Allah), Allah turned their hearts away (from the Right Path). And Allah guides not the people who are Fasigdn (rebellious, disobedient to Allah).” (As-Saff, 61:5)

AL-FAWA'ID : A Collection of Wise Syaings * 219

Allah, the Almighty aiso said, EDOLN SEU py sb JEON UL Sp

which means, “Nay! But on their hearts is the Ran (covering of sins and evil deeds) which they used to earn.” (Al-Mutaffifin,

Allah, the Exalted said that their sins and evil deeds covered their hearts and came in between them and faith in His verses, therefore, they said that these verses were nothing but tales of the ancients.

Allah, the Almighty said about the hypocrites, 4. HES TIS. >

which means, “They have forgotten Allah, so He has forgotten them.” (At-Tawbah, 9:67)

He rewarded them for their indifference to Him by forgetting (neglecting) them, and did not mention any guidance or mercy concerning them. He also said that He made them forget themselves, therefore, they did not try to make themselves perfect by means of good knowledge and righteous deeds, which are guidance and the religion of truth (islam). He made them forget to seek after that, to love it, learn it and aspire after it, as a punishment for their indifference to Him. The Almighty said the following verses concerning them,

which means, “Such are men whose hearts Allah has sealed, and they follow their lusts (evil desires). While as for those who

220 * AL-FAWA'ID: A Collection of Wise Sayings

accept guidance, He increases their guidance, and bestows on them their piety.” (Muhammad, 47:16-17)

He combined following one’s vain desires with aberration, which is its outcome and its cause, just as He connected piety and guidance for those who are guided.

Chapter: Guidance is the Partner of Mercy and Aberration is the Partner of Misery

Just as Allah, the Almighty connects guidance and piety, and aberration and transgression, in the same way, He connects guidance, mercy, aberration and distress. Concerning the first, Allah, the Almighty said, ee Lee wt “eo Fea ae 4) ole oe ls BO oe Ge Ue OLS! >

which means, “They are on (true) guidance from their Lord, and they are the successful.” (Al-Baqarah, 2:5)

which means, “They are those on whom are the Salawét (i.e. blessings, etc.) (i.e. who are blessed and will be forgiven) from their Lord, and (they are those who) receive His Mercy, and it is they who are the guided-ones.” (Al-Baqarah, 2:157)

And He, the Almighty said concerning the believers, Reds Goll yn WI edg aay BS ob ES I >

AL-FAWA'ID : A Collection of Wise Syaings * 221

which means, “(They say): "Our Lord! Let not our hearts deviate (from the truth) after You have guided us, and grant us mercy from You. Truly, You are the Bestower." (Al-'Imran, 3:8)

And the people of the cave said,

40S bs Bal Se pay TK) dd yy tone WS... p which means, "Our Lord! Bestow on us mercy from Yourself, and facilitate for us our affair in the rignt way!" (Al-Kahf, 18:10) And He, the Almighty said,

obs at Fe sh lc ne 2 BQ, O95 ace fee wc

which means, “Indeed in their stories, there is a lesson for men of understanding. It (the Qur'an) is not a forged statement but a confirmation of the Allah's existing Books [the Taurat (Torah), the Injeei (Gospel) and other Scriptures of Allah] and a detailed explanation of everything and a guide and a Mercy for the people who believe.” (Ydsuf, 12:111)

which means, “And We have not sent down the Book (the Qur'an) to you (O Muhammad), except that you may explain clearly unto them those things in which they differ, and (as) a guidance and a mercy for a folk who believe.” (An-Nahl, 16:64)

And He, the Almighty said,

222 * AL-FAWA'ID: A Collection of Wise Sayings

- ay Bate ao Doe Fa NER ee ree erin pee

which means, “And We have sent down to you the Book (the Qur'an) as an exposition of everything, a guidance, a mercy, and glad tidings for those who have submitted themselves (to Allah as Muslims).” (An-Nahl, 16:89)

And He, the Almighty said,

which means, “O mankind! There has come to you a good advice from your Lord (i.e. the Quran, ordering all that is good and forbidding all that is evil), and a healing for that (disease of ignorance, doubt, hypocrisy and differences, etc.) in your breasts, - a guidance and a mercy (explaining lawful and unlawful things, etc.) for the believers.” (YGnus, 10:57)

Then Allah, the Almighty mentioned it again,

which means, “Say: he "In the Bounty of Allah, and in His Mercy (i.e. Iskam and the Qur'an); -therein let them rejoice.” (Ydnus,

Verily, there are various expressions of the predecessors concerning the interpretation of the words (Bounty) and (Mercy). But the truth is that they refer to guidance and grace. His Bounty is His Guidance, and His Mercy is His Grace. That is why He connects guidance and grace, just like in Al-Fatihah,

which means, “Guide us to the Straight Way. The Way of those on whom You have bestowed Your Grace.” (Ai-Fatihah, 1:5-6)

The likeness of this is His word to His Messenger (peace be upon him) while reminding him of His Grace upon him.

which means, “Did He not find you (0 Muhammad (Peace be upon him)) an orphan and gave you a refuge? And He found you unaware (of the Qur'an, its legal laws, and Prophethood, etc.) and guided you? Asnd He found you poor, and made you rich (self-sufficient with self-contentment, etc.)?” (Ad-Duha,

He gathered His guidance and Grace upon him, by giving him refuge and helping him.

which means, “Tell me, if | have a clear proof from my Lord, and a Mercy (Prophethood, etc.) has come to me from Him.” (Hid,

which means, “Tell me, if | have a clear evidence from my Lord, and He has given me a good sustenance from Himself (shall | corrupt it by mixing it with the unlawfully earned money).” (Hid,

which means, “Then they found one of Our slaves, unto whom We had bestowed mercy from Us, and whom We had taught knowledge from Us.” (Al-Kahf, 18:65)

which means, “Verily, We have given you (O Muhammad) a manifest victory That Allah may forgive you your sins of the past and the future, and complete His Favour on you, and guide you on the Straight Path. And that Allah may help you with strong help.” (Al-Fath, 48:1-3)

AN oi 85 1 SS ie oils USL Cost alle A Uph... p which means, “Allah has sent down to you the Book (The Qur'an), and Al-Hikmah (Islamic laws, knowledge of legal and illegal things i.e. the Prophet's Sunnah - legal ways), and taught

you that which you knew not. And Ever Great is the Grace of Allah unto you (O Muhammad).” (An-Nisa’, 4:113)

And He, the Almighty said,

which means, “And had it not been for the Grace of Allah and His Mercy on you, not one of you would ever have been pure from sins." (An-Ndr, 24:21)

Therefore, His Bounty is His Guidance, and His Mercy is His Grace and Kindness to them.
content_id:
Allah Yang Maha Tinggi berfirman,

= Peete on Te ns Ce a CB pS Ly 85 Ml EG ua 4 SI Lh >

yang artinya, "Maka mengapa kamu terpecah menjadi dua golongan mengenai orang-orang munafik? Allah telah mengembalikan

mereka (kepada kekufuran) disebabkan apa yang telah mereka peroleh."

yang artinya, "Dan mereka berkata, 'Hati kami tertutup (yakni tidak mendengar atau memahami Firman Allah).' Tidak demikian, Allah telah melaknat mereka karena kekufuran mereka, maka sedikit sekali dari mereka yang beriman." (Al-Baqarah, 2:88)

Allah Yang Maha Tinggi berfirman,

218 * AL-FAWA'ID: Kumpulan Kata-Kata Hikmah

a7 . > eo ous Po bore te ea 4B). 8 Ol ay La BI US pa pels apes Llaly >

yang artinya, "Dan Kami akan memalingkan hati dan penglihatan mereka (dari petunjuk), sebagaimana mereka menolak untuk beriman kepadanya pada kali pertama." (Al-An'am, 6:110)

Allah Yang Maha Tinggi mengabarkan bahwa Dia menghukum mereka karena kekufuran mereka ketika para Nabi datang kepada mereka membawa risalah. Mereka mengetahuinya dengan baik, namun mereka berpaling darinya, maka Allah Yang Maha Kuasa memalingkan hati dan penglihatan mereka dari petunjuk, dan menghalangi antara mereka dengan iman. Sebagaimana Allah Yang Maha Tinggi berfirman,

< fe ston ws rant 4¢@ oes eek de gk Lash igtlety pea es SES IY Lily ob tat Ite Gall GE > 4@) alls ST ou Ue al

yang artinya, "Wahai orang-orang yang beriman! Penuhilah seruan Allah dan Rasul-Nya apabila dia menyerumu kepada sesuatu yang memberi kehidupan kepadamu, dan ketahuilah bahwa Allah menghalangi antara seseorang dan hatinya (yakni Dia mencegah orang jahat untuk memutuskan sesuatu)." (Al-Anfal, 8:24)

Allah Yang Maha Tinggi memerintahkan mereka untuk menaati-Nya dan menaati Rasul-Nya (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) ketika Dia menyeru mereka kepada sesuatu yang memberi kehidupan kepada mereka. Kemudian Dia memperingatkan mereka agar tidak lalai menaati-Nya, karena hal itu akan menjadi sebab Dia menghalangi antara mereka dan hati mereka. Allah Yang Maha Tinggi berfirman,

yang artinya, "Maka ketika mereka berpaling (dari jalan Allah), Allah memalingkan hati mereka (dari jalan yang benar). Dan Allah tidak memberi petunjuk kepada kaum yang fasik (durhaka, tidak taat kepada Allah)." (Ash-Shaff, 61:5)

AL-FAWA'ID: Kumpulan Kata-Kata Hikmah * 219

Allah Yang Maha Kuasa juga berfirman, EDOLN SEU py sb JEON UL Sp

yang artinya, "Sekali-kali tidak! Bahkan hati mereka telah tertutup oleh Ran (penutup dosa dan perbuatan buruk) yang biasa mereka kerjakan." (Al-Muthaffifin,

Allah Yang Maha Tinggi menyatakan bahwa dosa-dosa dan perbuatan buruk mereka telah menutupi hati mereka dan menghalangi antara mereka dengan iman kepada ayat-ayat-Nya, oleh karena itu mereka mengatakan bahwa ayat-ayat ini tidak lain hanyalah dongeng orang-orang terdahulu.

Allah Yang Maha Kuasa berfirman tentang orang-orang munafik, 4. HES TIS. >

yang artinya, "Mereka telah melupakan Allah, maka Allah melupakan mereka." (At-Tawbah, 9:67)

Dia membalas mereka atas kelalaian mereka terhadap-Nya dengan melupakan (mengabaikan) mereka, dan tidak menyebutkan petunjuk atau rahmat apa pun bagi mereka. Dia juga menyatakan bahwa Dia menjadikan mereka lupa terhadap diri mereka sendiri, sehingga mereka tidak berusaha menyempurnakan diri mereka dengan ilmu yang bermanfaat dan amal saleh, yang merupakan petunjuk dan agama yang benar (Islam). Dia menjadikan mereka lupa untuk mencarinya, mencintainya, mempelajarinya dan mengaspirasi kepadanya, sebagai hukuman atas kelalaian mereka terhadap-Nya. Allah Yang Maha Tinggi berfirman ayat-ayat berikut mengenai mereka,

Lr a ern a are? ee ee <t . Basis (Ach Cyl Ey Aaclgal ysl Ayyl Ue a ab ind Us. &

DAA BIER aie ee AES ghey Ce

yang artinya, "Mereka itulah orang-orang yang hatinya telah dikunci mati oleh Allah, dan mereka mengikuti hawa nafsu mereka (keinginan buruk). Adapun orang-orang yang

220 * AL-FAWA'ID: Kumpulan Kata-Kata Hikmah

menerima petunjuk, Allah menambah petunjuk kepada mereka dan memberikan ketakwaan kepada mereka." (Muhammad, 47:16-17)

Dia menggabungkan mengikuti hawa nafsu dengan kesesatan, yang merupakan akibat dan penyebabnya, sebagaimana Dia menghubungkan ketakwaan dan petunjuk bagi mereka yang mendapat petunjuk.

Bab: Petunjuk adalah Pasangan Rahmat dan Kesesatan adalah Pasangan Kesengsaraan

Sebagaimana Allah Yang Maha Kuasa menghubungkan petunjuk dan ketakwaan, serta kesesatan dan pelanggaran, dengan cara yang sama pula Dia menghubungkan petunjuk, rahmat, kesesatan dan kesengsaraan. yang artinya, "Mereka itulah yang berada di atas petunjuk dari Rabb mereka, dan merekalah orang-orang yang beruntung." (Al-Baqarah, 2:5)

Dan Dia Yang Maha Kuasa berfirman,

yang artinya, "Mereka itulah yang mendapat shalawat (yakni keberkahan, dan lain-lain) (yakni yang diberkahi dan akan diampuni) dari Rabb mereka, dan (mereka itulah yang) mendapat rahmat-Nya, dan mereka itulah orang-orang yang mendapat petunjuk." (Al-Baqarah, 2:157)

yang artinya, "(Mereka berdoa): 'Ya Rabb kami! Janganlah Engkau jadikan hati kami condong (kepada kesesatan) sesudah Engkau memberi petunjuk kepada kami, dan karuniakanlah kepada kami rahmat dari sisi-Mu. Sesungguhnya Engkaulah Maha Pemberi.'" (Ali 'Imran, 3:8)

yang artinya, "Sungguh pada kisah-kisah mereka terdapat pelajaran bagi orang-orang yang berakal. Al-Quran itu bukanlah cerita yang dibuat-buat, melainkan pembenaran (kitab-kitab) yang sebelumnya [Taurat, Injil dan Kitab-Kitab Allah lainnya] dan penjelasan segala sesuatu, serta petunjuk dan rahmat bagi kaum yang beriman." (Yusuf, 12:111)

yang artinya, "Dan Kami tidak menurunkan Kitab (Al-Quran) ini kepadamu (wahai Muhammad), melainkan agar engkau dapat menjelaskan kepada mereka apa yang mereka perselisihkan, serta menjadi petunjuk dan rahmat bagi kaum yang beriman." (An-Nahl, 16:64)

- ay Bate ao Doe Fa NER ee ree erin pee

yang artinya, "Dan Kami turunkan Kitab (Al-Quran) kepadamu untuk menjelaskan segala sesuatu, sebagai petunjuk, rahmat dan kabar gembira bagi orang-orang yang berserah diri (kepada Allah sebagai Muslim)." (An-Nahl, 16:89)

Dan Dia Yang Maha Kuasa berfirman,

yang artinya, "Wahai manusia! Sungguh telah datang kepadamu nasihat yang baik dari Rabb-mu (yakni Al-Quran, yang memerintahkan segala kebaikan dan melarang segala keburukan), dan penyembuh bagi penyakit (kebodohan, keraguan, kemunafikan dan perselisihan, dan lain-lain) yang ada dalam dadamu, serta petunjuk dan rahmat (yang menjelaskan perkara halal dan haram, dan lain-lain) bagi orang-orang yang beriman." (Yunus, 10:57)

Kemudian Allah Yang Maha Kuasa menyebutkannya kembali,

yang artinya, "Katakanlah: 'Dengan karunia Allah dan rahmat-Nya (yakni Islam dan Al-Quran), hendaklah dengan itu mereka bergembira.'" (Yunus,

Sesungguhnya terdapat berbagai ungkapan dari para pendahulu (salaf) mengenai penafsiran kata (karunia) dan (rahmat). Namun yang benar adalah bahwa keduanya merujuk kepada petunjuk dan anugerah. Karunia-Nya adalah petunjuk-Nya, dan rahmat-Nya adalah anugerah-Nya. Itulah mengapa Dia menghubungkan petunjuk dan anugerah, sebagaimana dalam Al-Fatihah,

AL-FAWA'ID: Kumpulan Kata-Kata Hikmah * 223

yang artinya, "Tunjukilah kami jalan yang lurus. (Yaitu) jalan orang-orang yang telah Engkau beri nikmat kepada mereka." (Al-Fatihah, 1:5-6)

Perumpamaan hal ini adalah firman-Nya kepada Rasul-Nya (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) seraya mengingatkannya akan nikmat-Nya kepadanya.

content_ar:
يُقرر ابن القيم رحمه الله في هذا الفصل قانوناً إلهياً محكماً: أن الهداية قرينة الرحمة، والضلال قرين الشقاء. فقد ربط الله تعالى بين الهدى والتقوى ربطاً لازماً في آيات كثيرة، كما ربط بين الكفر والضلالة والشقاء. ويستشهد الإمام بطائفة من الآيات القرآنية تُبيّن كيف أن الله يجزي أهل الإعراض بالإعراض عنهم، فيُختم على قلوبهم وتُصرف أبصارهم عن الهدى كما صرفوا وجوههم عنه. قال تعالى: ﴿فَلَمَّا زَاغُوا أَزَاغَ اللَّهُ قُلُوبَهُمْ﴾، فالإزاغة الإلهية جزاء على الزيغ الإنساني.

ثم يُبيّن ابن القيم أن الله تعالى جمع في بعض آياته بين الهدى والرحمة جمعاً لا انفصال فيه، فقال تعالى: ﴿أُولَٰئِكَ عَلَيْهِمْ صَلَوَاتٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِمْ وَرَحْمَةٌ وَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْمُهْتَدُونَ﴾، وقال: ﴿رَبَّنَا لَا تُزِغْ قُلُوبَنَا بَعْدَ إِذْ هَدَيْتَنَا وَهَبْ لَنَا مِن لَّدُنكَ رَحْمَةً﴾. وفي هذا دلالة على أن طلب الثبات على الهدى يستلزم طلب الرحمة من الله، لأن القلب وحده لا يثبت بنفسه بل هو بين إصبعين من أصابع الرحمن يُقلّبه كيف يشاء.

ويختتم الإمام بأن من أعظم آيات الكون المُرشدة إلى الله: تناسق الخلق وانتظامه وأنه لا يقوم بنفسه. فالجاهل ينظر إلى الكون ويرى المظاهر دون الحقائق: ﴿يَعْلَمُونَ ظَاهِرًا مِّنَ الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا وَهُمْ عَنِ الْآخِرَةِ هُمْ غَافِلُونَ﴾، أما المؤمن فينظر بنور الإيمان فيرى وراء الأسباب الحكيمَ المدبّر، ويزداد في كل آية كونية إيماناً وتسليماً.
